front: What are the three primary categories of e-waste based on their source?
back: Household e-waste (from consumers), Business/Industrial e-waste (from offices and industries), and Imported e-waste (shipped from other countries for processing or dumping).

front: Define ICT (Information and Communication Technology) waste and provide three common examples.
back: E-waste generated from devices used for computing, networking, and communication. Examples include computers, laptops, smartphones, routers, and printers.

front: What is Consumer Electronics e-waste? Name three typical items in this category.
back: E-waste from electronic devices used for entertainment and household functions. Examples include televisions, audio systems, DVD players, and gaming consoles.

front: Identify a major source of Large Household Appliance e-waste and list three examples.
back: E-waste from large, typically non-portable, appliances found in homes. A major source is the replacement of old models. Examples include refrigerators, washing machines, air conditioners, and microwave ovens.

front: What distinguishes Hazardous e-waste from other categories, and what are two dangerous components often found within it?
back: It is e-waste that contains significant amounts of toxic materials, requiring special handling for safe disposal. Dangerous components include cathode ray tubes (CRTs) found in old monitors/TVs (containing lead) and mercury found in fluorescent lamps and some switches.
